features.description: Add werk_core::ir::TensionList — flat attributed list IR. Define IrKind enum, Ir trait, Attributes struct (HashMap-backed with AttributeValue Number/Text/Bool/Categorical/Unknown), AttributeBuilder. Builder pulls every Part-IV registry attribute by joining store reads with computations from temporal.rs and projection.rs. space attribute populated from Ctx. urgency clamped 0..1 + urgency_raw exposed. gap_magnitude is binary 0/1. status categorical includes 'held' derived via Frontier. is_held bool also derived. Re-exports TensionList, IrKind, Attributes, AttributeValue from werk-core lib.rs.
preconditions: library/sigil-engine-decisions.md D1, D3, D4, D5, D6 are read
expectedBehavior: TensionList::build(store, tensions, ctx) returns Vec<TensionListEntry> where each entry has the full registry attribute set
AttributeBuilder is reusable; it accepts a list of attribute names and returns a populated Attributes map
Unknown attribute names are returned as Err with the unknown name in the message
All public types derive Debug + Clone; Attributes derives Serialize + Deserialize for cross-surface JSON

features.description: Add werk_core::ir::TensionTree — wraps Forest with a side-table HashMap<String, Attributes>. TensionTree::build(store, forest, ctx) joins computed attributes per node. Frontier::compute used once for the tree to derive is_held; status mapping per D1 (3 enum variants + 'held' derivation). Verifies TensionStatus enum is unchanged (no Held/Frozen variants added).
expectedBehavior: TensionTree::build returns a tree where every node has populated attributes
Root depths are 0; nested children depths match Forest::depth(...)
is_held=true for an active tension whose horizon range starts in the future
status='held' categorical for the same tension
urgency clamped to 1.0 for past-horizon tensions; urgency_raw exposed unclamped
TensionStatus enum is NOT modified (no Held or Frozen variants added)
verificationSteps: git diff werk-core/src/tension.rs (must show no enum changes)

features.description: Add werk_core::ir::AttributeGraph — wraps Forest::graph (FNX DiGraph) with node attributes. Translates edge type names: contains -> parent_child, merged_into -> merge_into, split_from unchanged. Unknown edge types pass through with diagnostics warn. Builder reuses the existing DiGraph from Forest::from_tensions_and_edges; layers attributes on top.
expectedBehavior: AttributeGraph::build returns a graph where every node has populated attributes
Edge attributes carry the design's edge_type names
Unknown edge types fall through unchanged and emit a Diagnostics warning

features.description: Add werk_core::ir::EpochSeries — per-tension time-indexed sequence backed by Store::get_epochs(id). Each EpochPoint carries timestamp + reconstructed snapshot (desire/actual/children). Cross-tension EpochSeries explicitly deferred (see D9; v1 supports Scope::Tension and Scope::Subtree { depth: 1 } only when paired with EpochSeries-consuming featurizers).
expectedBehavior: EpochSeries::for_tension(store, id) returns chronologically ordered series
Length matches Store::get_epochs(id)
Each point carries the snapshot from EpochRecord

features.description: Extend werk_core::address::Address with Sigil(i32) variant and *N parsing. Add Display impl for Sigil. Cross-space form werk:*7 supported via existing CrossSpace branch. *N does not collide with #, g:, s: prefixes. Round-trip tests via Display + parse.
expectedBehavior: parse_address("*7") returns Address::Sigil(7)
parse_address("werk:*7") returns CrossSpace { space: "werk", inner: Sigil(7) }
parse_address("#*7") is rejected
parse_address("g:*7") is parsed as Gesture (sigil prefix has lower precedence)
Display roundtrip is stable for *N and werk:*N

features.description: Add the sigils index table to Store::create_schema() (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sigils ...) plus indexes on short_code and logic_id. Add Store helper methods: record_sigil(SigilRecord), list_sigils() -> Vec<SigilRecord>, get_sigil_by_short_code(n), delete_sigil(n) -> Result<bool>. Stores file_path only (no SVG bytes). Update the schema docstring at store.rs:14-73.
expectedBehavior: Fresh in-memory store has the sigils table with documented schema
Indexes idx_sigils_short_code and idx_sigils_logic exist
record_sigil inserts; list_sigils returns chronologically; get_sigil_by_short_code retrieves
delete_sigil(n) returns Ok(true) first time, Ok(false) second time
delete_sigil does NOT delete the file at file_path (caller's responsibility)

features.description: Create the werk-sigil crate skeleton. Add werk-sigil to workspace Cargo.toml members. Author lib.rs, engine.rs, ctx.rs, scope.rs, logic.rs, sigil.rs, ir.rs, error.rs. Define Engine type with render(scope, logic, ctx) -> Result<Sigil, SigilError> signature. Define stage traits (Selector, Featurizer<I>, Encoder<I>, Layouter<I>, Stylist, Renderer). Define Ctx with now + store + workspace_name + seed + ChaCha rng + diagnostics. Define IrKind enum mirroring werk-core. Default-seed derivation via blake3(canonical_inputs). Engine::render is pure (no I/O at boundary).
preconditions: library/sigil-engine-decisions.md D7 (rhai), D8 (inline glyphs), D9 (per-tension EpochSeries) read
expectedBehavior: werk-sigil compiles with no implementations beyond the skeleton (placeholder ConstantStylist + NoOpRenderer return early)
Engine::render can be invoked (with placeholder stages) and produces deterministic empty SVG
seed derivation is deterministic: same canonical inputs -> same seed
Two renders of the same (scope, logic) with seed=None produce byte-identical SVGs
Different scopes produce different seeds and different SVG bytes
No filesystem writes during render (verifiable: render against read-only tempdir)

features.description: Implement SvgRenderer in werk-sigil. Uses quick-xml or similar for streaming SVG construction. Embeds <metadata><werk-sigil> block with scope, logic, seed, generated, warnings count. Diagnostics from Ctx materialize into the warnings block. SvgBytes type is a thin wrapper over Vec<u8> with a write_to_path helper. Tests: golden test for an empty scene; metadata structural assertion; warnings count when ctx.diagnostics has entries.
expectedBehavior: Empty scene renders to a well-formed SVG with metadata block
<metadata><werk-sigil> block contains <scope>, <logic>, <seed>, <generated>, <warnings count='N'>
Diagnostics from Ctx serialize into <warning> entries inside the warnings block
ctx.now (not Utc::now()) is used for the <generated> timestamp

features.description: Implement Selector::Subtree { root, depth } in werk-sigil/src/stages/selector.rs. Resolves the abstract scope to a concrete set of tension IDs (root + descendants up to depth, inclusive). Hands back a ResolvedScope containing the IDs and the workspace context. Other selector kinds (Tension, Space, Union, Query) implemented as thin shims; Query falls back to a basic predicate over a small set of fields (active, overdue, status=...).
expectedBehavior: Subtree { root: id, depth: 4 }.select returns root + descendants up to depth 4 inclusive
Depth-5 descendants are excluded
Subtree.select returns Err if root id not found in store

features.description: Implement Featurizer::TensionTree in werk-sigil/src/stages/featurizer.rs. Reads werk-core via Ctx::store, calls werk_core::ir::TensionTree::build, returns the IR. Reads the requested attribute list from the preset's [featurizer.tension_tree.attributes] section; emits only those attributes. Custom (non-registry) attributes pass through as AttributeValue::Unknown so encoders can read them by name.
expectedBehavior: Featurizer::TensionTree.featurize returns a TensionTree IR with attributes matching the requested list
Custom attributes pass through with AttributeValue::Unknown
Attributes not requested are omitted (not just empty)

features.description: Implement Encoder::StructuralDefault in werk-sigil/src/stages/encoder.rs. For each IR node, emits a MarkSpec with sensible defaults: primitive=circle for active, glyph for resolved, polygon for released, ellipse for held; r monotone with urgency; stroke_width monotone with gap_magnitude; fill_opacity monotone with depth (decreasing). Channel values come from the registry, not raw attribute values, so semantics are stable.
expectedBehavior: StructuralDefault produces one MarkSpec per IR node
primitive matches expected per status
r is monotonically increasing with urgency
stroke_width is monotonically increasing with gap_magnitude (binary 0/1 -> two values)
fill_opacity is monotonically decreasing with depth

features.description: Implement Layouter::RadialMandala in werk-sigil/src/stages/layouter.rs. Concentric rings, root at canvas center, depth -> radius. Angular share modes: child_weighted (default), equal, urgency_weighted. Inner padding (radians) between siblings. Structural marks: parent_child_curves (arc), ring_guides (dashed), spokes (optional). respect_status reduces angular share for held/released. Reads its parameters from [layouter.radial_mandala] section of the preset.
expectedBehavior: Root mark placed at canvas center frame
Depth-1 children at ring radius 80 (default ring_step)
Depth-2 children at ring radius 160
child_weighted distributes angular share by descendant_count
Structural marks (curves, ring guides) emitted per preset config

features.description: Implement Stylist::InkBrush in werk-sigil/src/stages/stylist.rs. Applies palette (ink_on_cream by default), background fill, line jitter, brush pressure variance, texture overlay via SVG <filter> chain (turbulence + displacement). filter_mode supports filter | cheap | off. Cheap mode pre-perturbs path coordinates; filter mode emits an SVG <filter> element.
expectedBehavior: After InkBrush, data marks have fill='#1a1818'
Background is '#f5efe1'
filter_mode='filter' produces a <filter> element in the SVG
filter_mode='off' produces no <filter> element and no jitter perturbation

features.description: Implement TOML schema + load_preset in werk-sigil/src/toml_schema.rs. Schema crystallizes against contemplative.toml. Required sections: [meta], [pipeline]. Optional: [scope], [featurizer.*], [encoder.*], [encoder.channels.*], [layouter.*], [stylist.*], [render], [seed]. Returns a Logic struct with fully-resolved stage references. Uses serde + a thin custom validator. Logic-construction errors are loud (line/col where possible) and return SigilError::Construction.
expectedBehavior: Loading werk-sigil/presets/contemplative.toml succeeds with zero diagnostics warnings
Loading a TOML missing [meta] returns SigilError::Construction with section info
Loading a TOML missing [pipeline] returns SigilError::Construction
Loading a TOML with unknown registry channel name returns SigilError::Construction (UnknownChannel)
Loading a TOML pairing a tree-requiring layouter with AttributeGraph featurizer returns SigilError::IrIncompatible

features.description: Wire everything together: invoke Engine::render against the bundled contemplative.toml on a fixture 7-node subtree (built in werk-sigil/tests/fixtures/small_tree.rs). Pin the resulting SVG as werk-sigil/tests/snapshots/contemplative.svg. Implement render-time graceful skip: a featurizer node with synthetic urgency=None gets skipped, the rest renders, the warnings count is incremented in the metadata block. This is the proof-of-architecture milestone — the first actual sigil rendered. Worker MUST attach the rendered SVG to the handoff for orchestrator + user eyeball review.
preconditions: selector-subtree, featurizer-tension-tree, encoder-structural-default, layouter-radial-mandala, stylist-ink-brush, toml-schema-loader, engine-svg-renderer all completed
expectedBehavior: cargo test -p werk-sigil tests::pipeline_smoke passes
werk-sigil/tests/snapshots/contemplative.svg is committed and matches byte-for-byte
SVG contains >= 7 data marks (one per tension) plus structural marks
Provenance metadata block contains scope, logic, seed, generated, warnings count
Render-time graceful skip works: synthetic missing data on one node skips it, warnings count incremented
Worker copies the rendered SVG into validation/m2/eyeball/contemplative.svg for user review
verificationSteps: open werk-sigil/tests/snapshots/contemplative.svg in an SVG viewer; confirm it looks like a recognizable mandala
Manual: orchestrator presents the SVG to the user; user approval recorded in validation/m2/eyeball/REVIEW.md

features.description: Implement Encoder::ShapeByStatus in werk-sigil/src/stages/encoder.rs. Categorical primitive choice driven by status: circle | ellipse | glyph | polygon. When primitive=glyph, glyph_family defaults to 'alchemical' and glyph_index = (short_code_hash % family_size). Tested against fixture with all 4 statuses present.
expectedBehavior: ShapeByStatus produces circle for active, ellipse for held, glyph for resolved, polygon for released
Glyph indices are deterministic (same short_code -> same index)

features.description: Implement Encoder::TomlDeclarative + the Rhai expression evaluator (D7). Reads channel mappings from [encoder.channels.*]. Three channel value forms: literal, field reference (E2: { field, scale, range, domain }, scale = linear|sqrt|log), and expression (E3: { expr = '...' }). Categorical mappings via { field, kind: 'categorical', mapping: {...} }. Rhai engine in expression-only mode (compile_expression). Compile errors are loud at logic-construction; eval errors at render time skip the element with a diagnostic. Add rhai dep to werk-sigil/Cargo.toml: rhai = { version = '1', default-features = false, features = ['std', 'sync'] } (or appropriate trim).
expectedBehavior: Literal channel values produce correct output
E2 field refs with sqrt scale produce correct mapped values
E2 field refs with linear/log scales work
E3 expressions evaluate sqrt, ln, +, *, -, /, %, |, &&, ==, comparison ops
Categorical mappings match the design example in contemplative.toml [encoder.channels.primitive]
Malformed expression (e.g., 'sqrt(urgency +') returns SigilError::Construction with line/col at logic-construction time
Eval error on one node (expression references attr not present on that node) skips the node with a diagnostic warning; other nodes render

features.description: Implement Layouter::FractalBranch in werk-sigil/src/stages/layouter.rs. Recursive branching tree placement. Each level scales geometry by configurable factor (default 0.7); leaves placed at fixed angles relative to parent. Deterministic per seed: same scope/logic/seed -> same layout. No two marks overlap (asserted in tests via bounding-box intersection check).
expectedBehavior: 4-level tree places leaves at decreasing scales
No two marks overlap (bbox check)
Root frame at canvas center

features.description: Implement Layouter::Constellation in werk-sigil/src/stages/layouter.rs. Force-directed scatter with edge bundling. Default for AttributeGraph scopes. Deterministic per seed via seeded RNG. Convergence: max 200 iterations or until total movement < epsilon. Edge bundling: edges of same type are pulled toward common medial paths (simple FD bundling, not full Holten).
expectedBehavior: 10-node graph with mixed edge types produces stable layout
Same seed -> same layout (deterministic)
parent_child-connected nodes cluster (centroid distance lower than mixed)

features.description: Implement Layouter::Grid in werk-sigil/src/stages/layouter.rs. Tiles sub-sigils for the Sheet combinator. For Union scopes, divides the canvas into a near-square grid (cols = ceil(sqrt(n)), rows = ceil(n/cols)). Each cell becomes a child Frame; the cell's content is a sub-sigil (rendered by the Sheet combinator at M6, but Grid layouter must work standalone with provided sub-marks).
expectedBehavior: 4-element Union produces a 2x2 tiled SVG
9-element Union produces a 3x3 tiled SVG
Each cell's sub-sigil is positioned within its own Frame

features.description: Implement Stylist::MinimalLine in werk-sigil/src/stages/stylist.rs. Clean monochrome linework: stroke-only data marks (fill='none'), no SVG <filter>, no background texture. Doubles as terminal-fallback approximation when that backend lands.
expectedBehavior: After MinimalLine, all data marks have fill='none'
No <filter> element in the SVG
Palette is monochrome (single stroke color)

features.description: Implement Stylist::Glyphic in werk-sigil/src/stages/stylist.rs. Heavy glyph substitution and formal symmetry. Forces primitive=glyph for nodes that can be glyphified (any with a glyph_family channel). Symmetric layouts get a mirrored <g> wrapper when preset enables it.
expectedBehavior: Majority of data marks render as <g class='glyph'> groups after Glyphic
Symmetric option produces a mirror group when enabled

features.description: Implement three glyph families as inline SVG path data in werk-sigil/src/glyphs/. Per D8, no external assets — all glyphs are Rust constants (e.g. const ALCHEMICAL_SUN: &str = 'M ... L ...';). Alchemical: minimum 16 distinct glyphs (target ~50 if budget allows; planetary, elemental, processual symbols). Geomantic: exactly 16 figures (Acquisitio, Amissio, Albus, ..., Via — derived from binary 4-row dot patterns). Hand-drawn primitives: minimum 12 brush atoms (target ~30 if budget allows). Each family implements GlyphFamily trait with glyph(idx: usize) -> SvgPath.
expectedBehavior: AlchemicalFamily::glyph(idx) returns distinct path data for at least 16 indices
GeomanticFamily::glyph(idx) returns one of the 16 canonical geomantic figures (verified against published reference patterns in test)
HandDrawnFamily::glyph(idx) returns distinct, non-empty path data for at least 12 indices
Same idx returns same path data on repeated calls (deterministic)
Index out of range wraps modulo and emits a diagnostic warning

features.description: Author werk-sigil/presets/glance.toml per design Part V (Space(active) + RadialMandala + MinimalLine + ShapeByStatus). Compact, fits in werk-tab. Pin golden snapshot werk-sigil/tests/snapshots/glance.svg.
expectedBehavior: glance.toml loads with zero warnings
Renders against a fixture Space(active) and produces a non-empty SVG with at least 4 data marks
Golden snapshot pinned and matches byte-for-byte

features.description: Author werk-sigil/presets/snapshot.toml per design Part V (Space(all) + RadialMandala + Glyphic + StructuralDefault). Pin golden snapshot werk-sigil/tests/snapshots/snapshot.svg. Visibly uses Glyphic stylist.
expectedBehavior: snapshot.toml loads with zero warnings
Majority of data marks are glyph elements after rendering
Golden snapshot pinned and matches byte-for-byte

features.description: Author werk-sigil/presets/identity.toml per design Part V (Union(all_spaces) + FractalBranch + Glyphic + TomlDeclarative). Rich, signature-flavored. Demonstrates TomlDeclarative's E3 expression power.
expectedBehavior: identity.toml loads with zero warnings
Renders against a Union of fixture spaces
TomlDeclarative E3 expressions are exercised in this preset
Golden snapshot pinned and matches byte-for-byte

features.description: Author werk-sigil/presets/oracle.toml per design Part V (Query(active) + Constellation + InkBrush + ShapeByStatus). Surfaces overdue/stale via color emphasis. Pin golden snapshot werk-sigil/tests/snapshots/oracle.svg. After this feature lands, all 5 presets exist; orchestrator collects all 5 SVGs into validation/m3/eyeball/ for user review.
expectedBehavior: oracle.toml loads with zero warnings
Visibly uses Constellation layouter (force-directed positioning)
Golden snapshot pinned and matches byte-for-byte
Worker copies all 5 preset SVGs to validation/m3/eyeball/ and prepares a side-by-side review document
verificationSteps: Manual: orchestrator presents all 5 SVGs to user; explicit approval recorded in validation/m3/eyeball/REVIEW.md

features.description: Implement werk-sigil/src/archive.rs. archive_path(scope, logic, seed, now) -> ~/.werk/sigils/YYYY-MM-DD/<scope-slug>-<logic>-<seed>.svg. cache_path(scope, logic, seed, werk_state_revision) -> ~/.werk/sigils/cache/<blake3>.svg. cleanup_cache(retention_days: u32) removes files in cache/ older than N days; archive untouched. werk_state_revision = MAX(updated_at) over resolved scope's elements (uses werk-core's Store::get_last_mutation_timestamps batched). All paths under $HOME/.werk/sigils/ — workers respect the user's HOME.
expectedBehavior: archive_path uses YYYY-MM-DD from ctx.now (not Utc::now())
cache_path key includes werk_state_revision; mutating a tension in the scope changes the key
cleanup_cache(7) removes files older than 7 days in cache/; archive/ untouched
Both helpers create parent directories as needed (mkdir -p semantics)

features.description: Implement werk sigil subcommand in werk-cli/src/commands/sigil.rs. Positional SCOPE accepts addresses. Flags: --logic NAME|PATH (default 'contemplative'), --seed N, --out PATH, --save, --json, --dry-run. Wire into Cli enum at werk-cli/src/commands/mod.rs and dispatch in werk-cli/src/main.rs alongside Field. Add werk-sigil = { path = '../werk-sigil' } to werk-cli/Cargo.toml. JSON output struct: { scope, logic, logic_version, seed, path?, svg?, warnings: [] }. Errors via WerkError::error_code() mapping. Examples in #[command(after_help)]. --save calls archive helpers + records in sigils table. --save assigns a *N short code. Sigils do NOT enter the gesture log (verified by VAL-CROSS-005). Tests in werk-cli/tests/sigil.rs and werk-cli/tests/sigil_lifecycle.rs.
expectedBehavior: werk sigil 2 prints SVG to stdout, exits 0
werk sigil 2 --out /tmp/x.svg writes file, exits 0
werk sigil 2 --json prints structured JSON
werk sigil 2 --dry-run --out /tmp/x.svg does not create the file
werk sigil 2 --save writes archive + records in sigils table; new sigil has a *N short code
werk sigil 2 --seed 7 vs --seed 8 produces different output; same seed reproducible
Invalid scope returns exit 1 with JSON error code NOT_FOUND in --json mode
werk sigil --help shows >= 3 examples
Sigil rendering does NOT enter the gesture log (werk log #N shows no new sigil-related gesture)
After --save, werk show *N displays file_path, logic, seed, scope (relies on existing show command's address dispatch)
verificationSteps: Manual: cargo run -p werk -- sigil 2 --logic contemplative --out /tmp/eyeball.svg && open /tmp/eyeball.svg

features.description: Add GET /api/sigil to werk-web/src/lib.rs. Query params: scope, logic, seed (optional). Returns SVG bytes with Content-Type: image/svg+xml. Computes cache key, hits cache if present, renders + caches if miss. Uses werk-sigil::Engine. Add werk-sigil = { path = '../werk-sigil' } to werk-web/Cargo.toml. Verifies parity with CLI: same scope/logic/seed/state -> byte-identical output between web and CLI.
expectedBehavior: GET /api/sigil?scope=2&logic=contemplative returns 200 + image/svg+xml
Body starts with <?xml
First call renders and writes ~/.werk/sigils/cache/<hash>.svg; second call serves from cache (atime/mtime check or stat)
Missing scope returns 400 with JSON error
GET /api/sigil with same params is byte-identical to werk sigil CLI output (same werk-state)
verificationSteps: Manual: cargo run -p werk -- serve --port 3749 & curl 'http://localhost:3749/api/sigil?scope=2&logic=contemplative' -o /tmp/x.svg; lsof -ti :3749 | xargs kill

features.description: Add GET /api/sigil/stream to werk-web/src/lib.rs. Subscribes to the existing AppState.tx broadcast::Sender, filters BroadcastStream to kind=='sigil_invalidated'. Add a second state.tx.send(SseEvent { kind: 'sigil_invalidated' }) after each existing mutation handler emit (create_tension, update_desired, update_reality, resolve_tension, release_tension, reopen_tension). Tests use tokio test + futures-util to consume the stream.
expectedBehavior: GET /api/sigil/stream returns text/event-stream and stays open
After PATCH /api/tensions/<id>/desired completes, a sigil_invalidated SSE event arrives within 2 seconds
Existing /api/events stream still works for werk-tab's existing reload-on-any pattern (no breaking change)
verificationSteps: Manual: curl -N http://localhost:3749/api/sigil/stream &; trigger a mutation; observe event

features.description: Add sigil mode to werk-tab/. Vanilla JS / HTML / CSS only (D11). Third toggle button alongside space/field. New <section class='sigil'>. loadSigil() fetches GET /api/sigil?logic=glance and injects SVG. SSE: add es.addEventListener('sigil_invalidated', ...) on the existing EventSource — do NOT open a second connection. Reuse existing discoverApi() port range. Offline state mirrors existing pattern. Validated via agent-browser.
expectedBehavior: Header shows three toggles after page load
Click sigil toggle: sigil section visible, space/field hidden, single inline <svg> rendered
After triggering a mutation via curl, SVG visibly changes within 2s
Network panel shows exactly one EventSource connection regardless of mode toggling
When werk serve is down, sigil section shows offline banner consistent with other modes
verificationSteps: agent-browser: load extension, navigate, click sigil, screenshot
agent-browser: trigger mutation, screenshot again, compare visually + by hash
Manual: orchestrator collects screenshots into validation/m5/eyeball/REVIEW.md

features.description: Implement Sheet combinator in werk-sigil/src/stages/layouter.rs (or a new src/combinators.rs). SheetLogic carries an inner_logic name; when invoked, recurses by calling Engine::render(sub_scope, inner_logic) for each sub-scope in a Union scope, then composes via Grid layouter. Recursion-depth limit = 4; depth=5 returns SigilError::RecursionLimit { depth: 5 }.
expectedBehavior: SheetLogic with inner_logic='glance' and 4-element Union produces 4 sub-sigils tiled in a grid
Total element count >= sum of sub-sigil element counts
Nesting 4 deep is allowed; nesting 5 returns SigilError::RecursionLimit

features.description: Implement Composite combinator with Concentric rule in werk-sigil/src/combinators.rs. CompositeLogic carries Vec<(Scope, Logic)> and a CompositionRule. v1 ships only Concentric (outer pair on outer ring, inner pair on inner ring). Other rules (Overlay, SideBySide, Masked) explicitly return SigilError::Unsupported.
expectedBehavior: CompositeLogic { rule: Concentric, pairs: [(scope_a, logic_a), (scope_b, logic_b)] } produces an SVG with both sigils stacked concentrically
Element count >= sum of children
Other rules return SigilError::Unsupported with rule name in message

features.description: Implement render_animation in werk-sigil/src/animation.rs. Public entry: render_animation(scope, logic, axis, output, ctx) -> Result<AnimatedSigil, SigilError>. Implements SeedSweep axis (re-renders for each seed in [start, end) step step) and FrameSequence output (writes one SVG per frame to dir). EpochRange axis returns SigilError::Unsupported.
expectedBehavior: SeedSweep { 0..5, step: 1 } + FrameSequence writes 5 SVG files in seed order
Each file contains the correct seed in its <metadata><seed> block
EpochRange axis returns SigilError::Unsupported

features.description: Add ParamSweep axis to render_animation. ParamSweep { stage: StageRef, param: String, from: Value, to: Value, frames: usize } interpolates a single numeric stage parameter across N frames. Linear interpolation only in v1. AnimatedSvg output remains SigilError::Unsupported in v1.
expectedBehavior: ParamSweep { stage: layouter, param: 'ring_step', from: 60.0, to: 100.0, frames: 5 } produces 5 SVGs with values [60, 70, 80, 90, 100]
Each render is deterministic against its sweep value
AnimatedSvg output returns SigilError::Unsupported

features.description: Audit all error paths in werk-sigil and ensure the loud-vs-graceful discipline (design Part VIII) is consistently applied. Add tests proving: schema parse error loud at construct-time; expression parse error loud at construct-time; unknown channel name loud; IR-shape mismatch loud; missing-field-on-one-node graceful with diagnostic warning. Where any path currently panics or silently swallows, fix it.
expectedBehavior: All Construction-time errors return SigilError::Construction with location info
All render-time per-element issues result in skipped element + diagnostic warning
No panic in any rendering path (verified by panic-free fuzz test on 100 randomized fixture trees)

features.description: Wire cleanup_cache into the CLI startup and the web handler startup. Default retention_days = 7; configurable via WERK_SIGIL_CACHE_RETENTION_DAYS env var. Archive (~/.werk/sigils/YYYY-MM-DD/) is never auto-cleaned.
expectedBehavior: cleanup_cache(7) removes cache files older than 7 days
Newer cache files preserved
Archive untouched
WERK_SIGIL_CACHE_RETENTION_DAYS=1 honored

features.description: Add notify (v6) dep to werk-sigil; implement file watcher on werk-sigil/presets/ and any user-supplied logic dir. On file change, recompute logic_version (hash of file bytes); active subscribers to /api/sigil/stream get a sigil_invalidated event. Behind the workspace-default 'hot-reload' feature flag (D10) — disable for tests that don't exercise reload.
expectedBehavior: Modifying werk-sigil/presets/contemplative.toml causes logic_version to change
Active /api/sigil/stream subscriber receives sigil_invalidated within 1 second
With feature 'hot-reload' disabled, the watcher is not started
verificationSteps: Manual: cargo run -p werk -- serve --port 3749 &; curl -N http://localhost:3749/api/sigil/stream &; touch werk-sigil/presets/contemplative.toml; observe event; lsof -ti :3749 | xargs kill

features.description: Performance benchmark + optimization pass on Constellation layouter (the most expensive stage). Add a release-mode test that asserts contemplative render of a 50-tension subtree completes in < 100ms (median of 20 runs, std::time::Instant). If slow, profile with a lightweight approach (println! timings around stage boundaries) and optimize.
expectedBehavior: cargo test --release -p werk-sigil bench::tests::contemplative_50_node_under_100ms passes on a modern machine
If above bar: profile + optimize until below bar

features.description: Final mission gate: orchestrator collects all 5 preset SVGs (current state), prepares a side-by-side review document at validation/m6/eyeball/distinct-presets.md, and presents to user. User confirms presets are visually distinct. Update root README.md to mention the sigil engine briefly (one paragraph) per orchestrator end-of-mission discipline.
expectedBehavior: All 5 preset SVGs rendered against fixture; collected at validation/m6/eyeball/
Side-by-side review doc presents them clearly
User explicit approval recorded
README.md mentions the sigil engine in a one-paragraph summary
All assertions in validation-state.json are 'passed'
verificationSteps: Manual: orchestrator presents to user; approval in REVIEW.md
git diff README.md (must show new paragraph)
